SOURCE: 
Authors’ 
own 
elaboration. 103
A DEEP DIVE INTO TWO AGRICUL
TURE HUMAN CAPITAL INVESTMENT CASES


	
	 DESIGN BUILDING BLOCK
	 	
 
K  Targeting women livestock farmers, APS and MTs
As a target group, the 57 000 women are smallholder/livestock farmers with 
land holdings from 0.1 ha to 1.2 ha and all members of self-help groups. They 
are between 20–53 years of age, have education levels ranging from no 
schooling to graduates and are willing to invest in improving animal rearing 
practices. They are traditional animal farmers with livestock consisting of a 
few goats, cattle, pigs, hens and ducks. From the 57 000 female livestock farmers, each village selects APS 
based on eligibility criteria that include: (i) a minimum of nine years schooling; 
(ii) belonging to the same village; (iii) membership of a local producer group; 
(iv) being a livestock farmer with at least two animals; (v) willing to travel for 
training and exposure visits; and (vi) actively looking for increased cash income. MTs are all previous APS selected for further training according to the 
following criteria: (i) proven training skills based on earlier experience in farmer 
training; (ii) hands-on experience with livestock; (iii) female between 30–45 
years of age; (iv) education level of at least 10 years schooling; and (v) ability 
to travel to various parts of the state. MTs are all women in the 28–34-year-old group, all are smallholder 
farmers, holding around 1.5 acres of land and having 10–15 years of formal 
education. A few male livestock farmers are involved in the JOHAR project, however 
95 percent of trained ASP and thereby MTs are female. L  Method
The method of agriculture human capital development in the JOHAR project 
has two distinct components according to who the primary learner is: (i) how 
the APS and the MTs acquire their skills and capabilities; and (ii) how other 
livestock farmers do so. See Figure 5.2. How APS and MTs develop their agriculture human capital
To become an APS, MTs train the women in groups of 15–20. Each APS receives 
30 days training over 18 months in five sessions of 4–7 days each. After 
completing the third training session and six months’ experience, the APS is 
eligible to take the certification examination with further preparation from the 
Agriculture Skills Council of India over seven days. Certification offers many 
benefits, it professionalizes the APS and helps standardize their quality across 
the country. The cost of training an APS includes that for training an MT in 
trainer-of-trainer mode and is INR 75 000 (USD 975). This includes acco-
mmodation, travel expenses and all associated costs such as the services of 
Asset & W in preparing materials, training MTs and coaching support for APS. MTs are responsible for training the APS such as preparing the venue, planning 
the 6–7 days, deciding training content, sub-group activities, setting tests 
during training, feedback forms, field assignments, logistics and handling the 
participants’ urgent needs. MTs obtain additional certification, giving the added benefit that they 
are recognized as the most professional trainers and can work anywhere in 
India. To learn community facilitation skills and conflict resolution, they under-
go ten days special training with Asset & W.
104   
HOW TO INVEST IN FARMERS?
compressed_context:
H1: THEORY-OF-CHANGE-JOHAR  
INPUT: PROJECT-DESIGN-STRATEGIES IMPLEMENTATION  
OUTPUT: AGRICULTURE-HUMAN-CAPITAL DEVELOPED  
OUTCOME: FARMER-ACTIONS/BEHAVIOURS ADOPTED DUE-TO HUMAN-CAPITAL  
IMPACT: CHANGES RESULTING FROM ADOPTED ACTIONS ∧ BEHAVIOURS  

CTX: IMPLEMENTATION-ACTIONS  
  AGENT: TECHNICAL-SERVICE-PROVIDERS  
  ACTION: TRAIN APS/MTs AT TECHNICAL-SUPPORT-AGENCIES  
  SUPPORT-AGENCIES: AGRICULTURE-SKILL-COUNCIL-OF-INDIA CONDUCT CERT-EXAMS  
  ACTION: APS/MTs PROVIDE STRUCTURED-TRAINING AT TRAINING-CENTRES  
  ACTION: APS/MTs PROVIDE EXTENSION VIA FARMER-FIELD-SCHOOLS AT HOME  

CTX: SKILLS  
  TECHNICAL-AG-SKILLS: ANIMAL-CARE↑; CLEANLINESS(ANIMAL+SHED)  
  TECHNICAL-AG-SKILLS2: FEED-SUPPLEMENTS; CLEAN-DRINKING-WATER  
  FUNCTIONAL-SKILLS: VET-SERVICES-SEEKING; INCOME-EARNER-CONFIDENCE  
  FUNCTIONAL-SKILLS2: OUT-OF-HOME-TRAINING-CONFIDENCE; COMMUNICATION  
  BUSINESS-SKILLS: GOAT-SALES-BY-WEIGHT; REINVEST-IN-PRODUCTION  

CTX: APS/MT-SKILLS  
  PARA-VET-SKILLS: DEWORMING; NUTRITION; TEETH-CLIPPING; TIMELY-CASTRATION; ANIMAL-FIRST-AID  
  DIGITAL-SKILLS-1: COMPUTER-OPERATION; DIGITAL-CONTENT-USE; TRAINING-DESIGN; SESSION-PRESENTATION  
  DIGITAL-SKILLS-2: COMMUNICATION; SOCIETY-BETTERMENT-VALUING; GENDER-BIAS-HANDLING; GROUP-FACILITATION; TEACHING  
  BUSINESS-SKILLS(APS/MTs): LIVESTOCK-MARKETING-LINKAGES; ANIMAL-VALUATION-BY-WEIGHT; VET-SERVICE-PLAN; EXPERT-NETWORKING; BUSINESS-REINVESTMENT  

CTX: SKILLS-DEVELOPED  
  ACCESS(APS/MTs) → FARMER-REARING-PRACTICES↑  
  ACCESS(APS/MTs) → FARMER-INCOME REINVESTED-IN-PRODUCTION  
  FARMERS → NEGOTIATE-BETTER-PRICES ∧ MARKET-ENGAGEMENT↑  

CTX: HELP-SEEKING-BEHAVIOUR  
  APS/MTs → PROVIDE-PARA-VET-SERVICES BEYOND-JOHAR  
  APS/MTs → TEACH ∧ NETWORK ∧ LEAD ∧ CONDUCT-TRAININGS ∧ MODEL-GOOD-PRACTICES  
  APS/MTs → REINVEST-IN-OWN-BUSINESSES  
  APS/MTs → SEEK-BETTER-JOBS DUE-TO CERTIFICATION  

CTX: OUTCOMES  
  LIVESTOCK-PRODUCTION↑ ∧ PRODUCTIVITY↑  
  FARMER-INCOME↑ ∧ APS/MT-INCOME↑  
  WOMEN-FARMERS → SELF-CONFIDENCE ∧ EMPOWERMENT  
  APS/MTs → SELF-CONFIDENCE ∧ RECOGNITION ∧ EXPERT-ACCEPTANCE  
  COMMUNITY-NETWORKS → IMPROVED ∧ SUPPORTIVE  
  LIVESTOCK-BUSINESSES↑  

CTX: IMPACT-ADD-ONS  
  GRANTS → PRODUCER-GROUPS  
  ESTABLISH LIVESTOCK-SERVICE-CENTRES(RUN-BY PRODUCER-GROUPS) → APS/MTs PROVIDE INPUTS ∧ MEDICINE  

H2: TARGET-GROUP  
  TARGET: WOMEN-LIVESTOCK-FARMERS=57 000 ∧ LANDHOLDINGS=0.1–1.2ha ∧ SELF-HELP-GROUP-MEMBERS  
  ATTRIBUTES: AGE=20–53 ∧ EDUCATION=NONE–GRADUATE ∧ TRADITIONAL-ANIMAL-FARMERS  
  SELECTION: VILLAGES SELECT APS IF {SCHOOLING≥9YR; SAME-VILLAGE; PRODUCER-GROUP-MEMBER; LIVESTOCK≥2; WILLING-TO-TRAVEL; CASH-INCOME-SEEKING}  
  MT-DEFINITION: FROM APS SELECT IF {TRAINING-SKILLS-PROVEN; LIVESTOCK-HANDS-ON; AGE=30–45; SCHOOLING≥10YR; ABILITY-TO-TRAVEL}  
  MT-DEMOGRAPHICS: WOMEN AGE=28–34 ∧ LAND≈1.5ac ∧ SCHOOLING=10–15YR  
  GENDER-RATIO: APS/MTs FEMALE=95% ∧ MALE-APS=FEW  

H2: METHOD  
STEP1: APS/MT-SKILL-ACQUISITION  
  APS TRAIN GROUP(15–20): TOTAL=30-DAYS OVER 18-MONTHS IN 5 SESSIONS(4–7-DAYS)  
  AFTER SESSION3 + 6-MONTHS EXPERIENCE → APS ELIGIBLE-FOR CERT-EXAM  
  CERT-PREP: AGRI-SKILL-COUNCIL-OF-INDIA TRAINING=7-DAYS  
  CERTIFICATION ⇒ APS-PROFESSIONALIZATION ∧ QUALITY-STANDARDIZATION  
  COST(APs)=INR75 000(USD975) ∧ INCLUDES={ACCOMMODATION; TRAVEL; MATERIALS; MT-TRAINING; APS-COACHING}  
  MT RESPONSIBILITIES-1: VENUE-PREP; SESSION-PLANNING(6–7-DAYS); CONTENT-DECISION; SUB-GROUP-ACTIVITIES  
  MT RESPONSIBILITIES-2: IN-TRAINING-TESTS; FEEDBACK-FORMS; FIELD-ASSIGNMENTS; LOGISTICS; PARTICIPANT-NEEDS  
  MT CERTIFICATION ⇒ PROFESSIONAL-RECOGNITION ∧ INDIA-WIDE-MOBILITY  
  MT SPECIAL-TRAINING(ASSET-&-W)=10-DAYS FOR COMMUNITY-FACILITATION ∧ CONFLICT-RESOLUTION  

STEP2: FARMER-SKILL-ACQUISITION  
  APS/MTs → DELIVER-TRAINING ∧ EXTENSION VIA FARMER-FIELD-SCHOOLS ∧ TRAINING-CENTRES
